item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be sourced and is thus assumed to have not been released under CC 3.0. As such, it is assumed not compliant and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this to be pretty generic until the twist at the end. Totally made the article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hahaha, I've actually heard that song before but I didn't know the lyrics. That wasn't my intention when I wrote this but I love that I accidentally made that connection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any image inscribed on a surface by SCP-2865-2 with SCP-2865-3 will become an instance of SCP-2865-1 within 10 to 20 minutes. It is unknown if this anomalous effect is inherent to SCP-2865-2 or SCP-2865-3.

I think the answer is just that Foundation doesn't know much about this scenario for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is Jeremy considered anomalous and not a POI? As far as we know, the chalk piece could be the anomaly, and he is just the dick who found it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, how come no one caught onto the Pearl Jam reference? Granted, this is Jeremy the Terrible and his legacy can be erased from the blackboard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I'm so happy that you liked my piece enough to write this.

I really like it. You could probably expand this and post it as a tale, if you wanted to. Just cause I can't +1 a comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its dark in here.
Its always dark unles they need me too draw.

How long have I been down here? A year? A hundred years? They wont let me use the chalk to count days. They say its wastful. But the chalk never runs out.

The guard opens the door just enuf to slide throo a plate of something. I cant tell what it is, but I eat it anyway. Its the only food their is. I finish of the food with a cup of water. In the corner is a lump of rags. They might have fit a boy, but not a man.

I've tried to get out so many ways. I've tried drawing swords. I've tried drawing guns. I even tried drawing a car one time. Only they ever come to life. They always join the resistens. They never want to be friends.

A guard opens the door again to come get the plate. I catch him by suprise and knock him over. I grab his torch and run as fast as I can. I think I made it farther this time, but I still got caught. My hair is pulled back until Im staring my capter in the face. My voice, week from hunger and hoarse from disuse, only lets me wisper in defianse.

"Long live the king!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Reckoned".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's the original word of "rekined"?

I don't get a thing out of its original meaning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Risk management. Too many unknowns. Is it the chalk, or is the kid an early-manifest Reality Bender? Sure, dousing the area and grabbing the chalk would be a solid tactic IF the chalk is the anomalous item. BUT…

[data expunged]

…and that's why we don't go charging in hoses drenching.

Further, they did try using hoses; some still got away, if the chalk thefts are any indication. In my headcanon, they are either looking for another magic stick and/or they're using it for first aid.

+1 for the giant What If?! As mentioned above, GREAT child. Ok, great BRAT.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't quite get why this one is so hard to contain… the Addendum at the end shows that the instances can be neutralized with pressurized water. Why didn't the Foundation just send in a MTF armed with Super-Soakers to bag'n'tag the kid and his chalk from the start?

item.title: Image Team - Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This page's second image has been removed for not being CC compliant. Author, please contact myself or another member of the image team if you wish for assistance in finding a license-compatible image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, that makes more sense. I had assumed that Jeremy had actually stolen their only method of procreation in some way (the magic crayon), but it is somewhat more reasonable to assume that he did indeed create them as a way to escape his (in his opinion) depressing and boring world. Still a very interesting twist, with the power struggle between the 'republican' and 'monarchist' chalk creatures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 all the +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whenever I see a long SCP, I expect it to be bad unless you're a God at formulating yourself.

This one was like a movie to me.
The pictures are really good too, it's like I can imagine what everything looks like. Even though details about the castle and how -2 looked like - weren't given, (which I get why) the text as a whole is really interesting. Great tone, formal and not cliche as fuark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is how you do a first SCP. +1 from me, Malim. I expect more great SCP articles from you.

I'm not sure where you found the "bit of something something" that I said before this article was missing, but it's there now. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since the events of 2/11/20██, several dozen elementary schools and crafts stores in Montana have reported burglaries. In all cases nothing was stolen, except for large quantities of sidewalk chalk.

Sidewalk chalk civil war. I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If that's your interpretation that's perfectly valid, but my intention was that he created them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was good, but making the -1's not be beholden to the king's whim made it even better. I mean, this is just really fantastic, and everything an SCP article should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea.Your execution was superb. You can see how the child would come to this conclusion in the given circumstances and the power struggle that comes up leaves me wanting more. This is the sort of concept that I would read an entire book about.

This is my current favorite Series III scp, and it sets the bar pretty damn high for all the others.+1 from me and the praise of a low-ranking researcher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting concept, and very well executed as well. It has that air of childhood mystery and charm, but the eventual revolution provides a clever twist as well. Also, am I correct in thinking that the SCP implies that Jeremy had actually enslaved the chalk-creatures by way of his crayon?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That picture of the chalk-men…it's fantastic. If you found it, good find. If you made it, I want to see more out of you. Well-done article plus great picture makes me wish I could upvote more than once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remember, kids, don't break reality! Even if it gets you out of school, you'll end up held captive by the chalk-drawing monsters you've created and forced to create more and more of the things, and they won't even give you ice cream by way of apology.

This is good stuff - intriguing idea that gets developed fully and then piles interesting development on interesting development. I'd probably have upvoted anomalous Child-King of the Chalkmen, but each new development on that concept served to make it more and more interesting.

The depictions of all the characters were pretty believable, which is always good to see in an article about children.The Agents act like you'd expect without lurching into the ridiculously patronising tone you often see in stories like this. Jeremy the Terrible was better than a lot of fictional children, because my brain didn't scream 'this couldn't be a real child' the whole time.

Particularly, I love the humour of this piece, because it comes from taking an absurd premise and taking it exactly at face value in a relatively serious tone. It's much better than a lot of the 'funny' articles, because it's trying to do something other than goofiness and the comedy comes from more than just a ridiculous effect - it's a humour based on the occasional ridiculousness of real children. What's more, it's a pretty good humourous article but doesn't just try to be a joke, and I wish more people would try to write articles in such a vein.

All in all, a sterling job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it a lot. An interesting premise with good follow-through that kept my interest until the end. It's sort of like one of the kids from that chalktown escaped to strike out on his own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. Its a really good one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kid with magic object making entities and becoming king is rather cliched. Entities getting tired of the spoiled kid's shit and holding him as hostage is a nice change. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't it be followed by a [sic] then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It didn't say that the SCP itself was neutralized, it just said that SCP-2865-2 was. I guess it doesn't really make sense anymore. removing that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded. In fact, I'd almost call this Keter for its difficuilty of keeping this contained. Or at least the danger/risk of a small army of hundreds of chalk people making a run for it at once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this neutralized? There's still a king and loyal subjects, yes? And there are unaccounted for/uncontained rebels, yes? Where's the neutralization?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can dig this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was great in chat, and it's great now. Awesome job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this, ladies, gentlemen, and various other beings, is what is known as a spoiled brat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If anyone would pardon me for saying the following…

And the Shark Punching Centre was cordially invited to the court of King Jeremy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The SPC there was deliberate. Kid misheard the name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dear SPC

Was the note meant to be written as "SPC" or "SCP"?

Regardless, 'tis a fun read with an intriguing premise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A kid's imaginary friends stage a revolution. That's too good a concept to not upvote, and the execution is great as well. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a very intriguing article, you have my upvote :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it is theorized that SCP-2865-2 is being held in captivity by the "colony" as a means of reproduction.

This is where I upvoted. Long live the king.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Voct and Clef for helping me with this in chat.
